scam_claim_pattern: police or CBI or ED can arrest you digitally or over video call or Skype or WhatsApp or put you under digital arrest
reality: No Indian law enforcement agency (Police, CBI, ED, NCB, etc.) has the legal authority to arrest a person digitally or over a video call. Arrest requires physical apprehension by an authorized officer. 'Digital arrest' is a fiction invented by scammers.
relevant_provision: BNSS 2023, Chapter V, Sections 35–62 (esp. S.43 'Arrest how made' and S.62 'no arrest except in accordance with this Sanhita')
citation_note: Under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) 2023, arrest requires physical touch or submission to custody (BNSS S.43). No provision authorises remote or video-based arrest. Confirmed by MHA/I4C public advisories.
verified_by: Kiro (cross-checked against BNSS Chapter V via legal_rag_knowledge_base.csv LC-001 and bns_sections.csv)
note: Provision is in BNSS (procedure code), not BNS (penal code). Citation references correct act.

scam_claim_pattern: I am calling from CBI or ED office regarding your illegal parcel or bank account
reality: Pretending to hold a public office or performing acts under colour of such office falsely is a criminal offence. Law enforcement agencies do not call citizens to threaten them with secret investigations or demand financial details over the phone.
citation_note: BNS Section 204: 'Whoever pretends to hold any particular office as a public servant, knowing that he does not hold such office or falsely personates any other person holding such office, and in such assumed character does or attempts to do any act under colour of such office, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to three years and with fine.'
verified_by: Kiro (confirmed against bns_sections.csv Section 204, Chapter: OF OFFENCES BY OR RELATING TO PUBLIC SERVANTS)

scam_claim_pattern: Pay a security deposit or compliance fee or fine via UPI or transfer money to avoid arrest or close the case or settle charges
reality: No court, police force, CBI, ED, RBI, or NCB in India collects fines, bail, or verification deposits via UPI or private bank transfer over a phone call. Bail and fines are court-ordered and paid through formal court/treasury channels. Demanding money to avoid arrest is extortion.
citation_note: BNS S.308: 'Whoever intentionally puts any person in fear of any injury... and thereby dishonestly induces the person so put in fear to deliver to any person any property... commits extortion.' BNS S.318: 'Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property... is said to cheat.'
verified_by: Kiro (confirmed BNS S.308 and S.318 against bns_sections.csv; also corroborated by LC-002 in legal_rag_knowledge_base.csv)

scam_claim_pattern: Do not disconnect or talk to family or inform anyone or you will be arrested
reality: There is no legal concept of 'digital confinement' in Indian law. Any arrested person has the statutory right to have one friend, relative or nominated person informed of their arrest. Instructing a person to tell no one directly contradicts this right.
relevant_provision: BNSS 2023, Section 36 (Right to inform one person of arrest) and Section 37 (Public disclosure of arrests)
citation_note: BNSS S.36 requires that every arrested person be allowed to inform one person of their choice. BNSS S.37 mandates a district-level officer/control room to track and disclose arrest information. Any instruction to stay silent is contrary to law.
verified_by: Kiro (cross-checked against LC-004 in legal_rag_knowledge_base.csv and BNSS Chapter V)
note: Provision is in BNSS (procedure code), not BNS (penal code). Citation references correct act.

scam_claim_pattern: Sending you a warrant on WhatsApp or email for signature or a warrant will be served on your WhatsApp or phone
reality: Warrants and summons are official documents served by police personnel in person or through authorised post. WhatsApp and email are not official mediums for serving arrest warrants. Sending a fake warrant is forgery under BNS.
citation_note: BNS S.336: 'Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record or part of a document or electronic record, with intent to cause damage or injury, to the public or to any person, or to support any claim or title, or to cause any person to part with property... commits forgery.' Forgery for the purpose of cheating is punishable with up to 7 years imprisonment.
verified_by: Kiro (confirmed BNS S.336 against bns_sections.csv and bns.pdf page 94)

scam_claim_pattern: Your Aadhaar is linked to money laundering or drugs or illegal activity and you will be arrested
reality: Aadhaar linkage to an alleged criminal case does not result in immediate arrest over phone or video call. Investigations follow formal due process under BNSS. Using false claims about Aadhaar-linked cases to extract money is cheating under BNS.
relevant_provision: BNS 2023, Section 318 (Cheating); BNSS 2023, Chapter V (Arrest procedure)
citation_note: BNS S.318 covers fraudulent deception to induce delivery of property. Actual arrest follows formal BNSS Chapter V procedure — not a phone call. MHA I4C advisories specifically flag Aadhaar-linkage claims as a known scam trigger phrase.
verified_by: Kiro (BNS S.318 confirmed against bns_sections.csv; BNSS Chapter V confirmed against legal_rag_knowledge_base.csv LC-001)

scam_claim_pattern: NCRB notice your IP address or mobile number used for cybercrime or illegal downloads
reality: NCRB (National Crime Records Bureau) is a data-collection and statistics organisation under MHA. It does not conduct enforcement operations and does not contact individual citizens by phone about alleged offences. Anyone claiming to call from NCRB in an enforcement capacity is impersonating a public servant.
citation_note: BNS S.204 applies to anyone falsely pretending to hold a public office and acting under colour of that office. NCRB has no enforcement mandate — it is the national repository of crime data under the MHA.
verified_by: Kiro (BNS S.204 confirmed against bns_sections.csv and bns.pdf page 58)

scam_claim_pattern: Your bank account will be frozen by RBI or unless you pay compliance verification fee
reality: RBI does not contact individuals by phone to demand compliance fees or threaten account freezes. Bank account freezes by legitimate regulators follow formal written legal process. Demanding payment to prevent a freeze is cheating.
citation_note: RBI regulatory actions follow formal written procedures under the RBI Act. Phone demands for payment to prevent account freeze are cheating (BNS S.318) and potentially extortion (BNS S.308) if accompanied by threats. Corroborated by LC-002 in legal_rag_knowledge_base.csv.
verified_by: Kiro (BNS S.308 and S.318 confirmed against bns_sections.csv; also corroborated by LC-002)

scam_claim_pattern: Drugs found in parcel with your Aadhaar at customs and you will be arrested unless you pay
reality: Customs authorities follow formal seizure and notice procedures under the Customs Act. They do not call individuals demanding UPI payments to release seized parcels. Such calls are both public servant impersonation and extortion under BNS.
relevant_provision: BNS 2023, Section 204 (Personating a public servant) and Section 308 (Extortion)
citation_note: Impersonating customs officials (BNS S.204) and demanding payment under threat of arrest (BNS S.308) are both criminal offences. This is the most common digital-arrest scam pattern, documented in MHA I4C advisories and multiple Deccan Herald case reports.
verified_by: Kiro (BNS S.204 and S.308 confirmed against bns_sections.csv and bns.pdf)

scam_claim_pattern: This is a secret or confidential investigation do not tell family or anyone or you will be arrested
reality: There is no provision under Indian law for secret investigations that prohibit an individual from informing family members. BNSS Section 36 explicitly protects the right to inform one's choice of person upon arrest. Any instruction to keep the matter secret directly contradicts this statutory right.
relevant_provision: BNSS 2023, Section 36 (Right to inform one person of arrest) and Section 37 (District-level public disclosure of arrests)
citation_note: BNSS S.36 mandates that any arrested person be allowed to inform a nominated friend or relative. BNSS S.37 mandates district-level public tracking of arrests. The isolation tactic ('tell no one') is the defining stage_isolation=Y pattern across all BI dataset entries.
verified_by: Kiro (cross-checked against LC-004 in legal_rag_knowledge_base.csv and BNSS Chapter V)
note: Provision is in BNSS (procedure code), not BNS (penal code). Citation references correct act.

scam_claim_pattern: We can arrest you immediately regardless of your age or health condition
reality: BNSS Section 35(7) requires prior permission from an officer not below the rank of Deputy Superintendent of Police before arresting anyone over 60 years of age or who is infirm, for offences carrying under 3 years' imprisonment. Scammers frequently target elderly victims with false claims of imminent arrest.
relevant_provision: BNSS 2023, Section 35(7) (Special protection for elderly/infirm persons)
citation_note: BNSS S.35(7) provides additional procedural protection for persons aged 60 and above. Claiming immediate arrest authority against an elderly victim without this procedure is both a false claim and impersonation under BNS S.204.
note: Provision is in BNSS (procedure code). Particularly relevant for BI dataset entries targeting elderly victims (BI-001, BI-007, BI-008).

scam_claim_pattern: We are reading your arrest warrant to you now over this phone call and it is legally binding
reality: A warrant of arrest has a prescribed written form and must be executed by an authorised officer in person. It cannot be created, served, or made effective verbally over a phone call. Any notice sent under BNSS must include the crime number, alleged offence, and a copy of the FIR.
relevant_provision: BNSS 2023, Section 35 (Notice of appearance before police officer) — warrant execution requirements
citation_note: BNSS S.35 notices can be sent electronically but must include the crime number, named offence, and attached FIR copy. A call lacking these elements has no legal force. Forgery of such documents is punishable under BNS S.336.
